entities.title: A Cross Knight
entities.content: “New money. @[[b]Sir Miles Cross[/b]](person:70e226e5-5390-43d3-a466-388964cab7fc) may be the richest man in England, but he’s far from the fine breeding of his betters. He is surly and churlish, prone to arguments that can’t be won and foolish jabs at society. He doesn’t know his place, nor do his children.”

entities.title: A Foxy Duel for Nash
entities.content: “I’ve heard that @[[b]Lady Nash[/b]](person:e7354503-fa94-48d4-b042-7cc82d1f852a)’s husband, @[Sir Grover Nash](person:487073d6-53ef-4041-be07-a784e9bdf4ba), nearly dueled @[[b]Lt. Foxsmith[/b]](person:71777950-6f6e-41ce-896c-ce17f10a5752) once… I wonder for what. Of course he's dead now, but nobody ever would have called the old chap a duellist…”

entities.title: A Swift Pirate
entities.content: “He must have been a pirate. He must have been. No seaman has ever become that rich through honest work, I’ll bet on it. @[[b]Captain Swift[/b]](person:76a76a8b-0559-4164-af49-a1208d83a92f) plays the innocent, but just a merchant? Fah! He’s stolen from the crown. Must have.”

entities.title: A Swift Romance in a Davenport
entities.content: “@[[b]Lady Davenport[/b]](person:f28edc4e-305d-4dd9-b45d-a34dc0baec44) and @[[b]Captain Swift[/b]](person:76a76a8b-0559-4164-af49-a1208d83a92f) used to court, an age ago. That was before she married, of course. She’d promised to wait for him, but he was lost at sea for many months - and when he returned she’d thought him dead and married another. It was all quite the dramatic tragedy.”

entities.title: A Swift-Constructed Workhouse
entities.content: “It’ll be just another workhouse whatever @[[b]Captain Monty Swift[/b]](person:76a76a8b-0559-4164-af49-a1208d83a92f) builds, you mark my words. Nobody really cares about foundling children except what money they can make off of them. I don’t believe ‘Christian generosity’ as his reason for a minute.”

entities.title: Alice or Anne for Hastings
entities.content: “@[[b]Miss Alice Middleton[/b]](person:ea7fe390-1d1b-40a0-8566-ada617dbace8) is quite a sweet girl, but gets overshadowed by all her boisterous sisters. Kind to a fault, if not a bit silly. I’ve heard she and her sister @[[b]Miss Anne[/b]](person:9625c3b5-2e26-4844-9ae1-68bcc035878a) are both hoping @[[b]Mr. Hastings[/b]](person:e5a26d73-7dba-4e36-b5b5-535babc1766d) will court them, but that boy seems rather a mess, doesn’t he?”

entities.title: Alice's Poetry Habit
entities.content: “It’ll be just another workhouse whatever [b]Captain Monty Swift[/b] builds, you mark my words. Nobody really cares about foundling children except what money they can make off of them. I don’t believe ‘Christian generosity’ as his reason for a minute.”

entities.title: Ambitious Reverend
entities.content: “[b]Reverend Lewis[/b] is an ambitious young man. He has sharper eyes than any Reverend I ever met, and intends to marry well. I’d be surprised if he doesn’t have a keen handle on any matter of business that should fall into his lap.”

entities.title: Amelia's Fanny
entities.content: “[b]Miss Amelia Murray[/b] is almost too close with her new protege, [b]Miss Fanny Clark[/b]. Is there some reason Miss Murray hasn’t gotten married yet?”

entities.title: An Un-Swift Wind to Davenport
entities.content: “[b]Lady Davenport[/b] and [b]Captain Monty Swift[/b] courted when they were much younger. I wonder what brought about the end of that? Now that she’s widowed- well, he’s never come to call, not that I’ve heard of. Besides, at their age...?”

entities.title: Art of Chatsworth
entities.content: “A discerning art collector, the [b]Marquess of Chatsworth[/b]. His gallery is known to be one of the greatest in all Europe.”

entities.title: Arthur Pembroke's First Wife
entities.content: “I heard [b]Mr. Arthur Pembroke[/b]’s first marriage was nearly a failure in it’s own right. They might have separated if it weren’t for her unfortunate illness.”

entities.title: Barnyard Nash
entities.content: “I heard his father was disowned, and that [b]Sir Rawdon Nash[/b] was raised in a barn. Or was it a pig pen? Either way, a little polish can't cover up common.”

entities.title: Bruce Isn't Bright
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Bruce Willoughby[/b] is every ounce a gentleman. He also couldn’t logic his way out of a paper bag.”

entities.title: Bryon Broke Reputations and Got A Cavill
entities.content: “I heard the real reason that [b]Reverend Lewis[/b]’ brother Mr. Byron Lewis died was that he had a duel and left his wife, [b]Mrs. Lewis[/b], a widow. Over the honor of a girl he had ruined! Apparently, the girl had a babe too! These days, he’s all grown up and ready to come into his inheritance… Who is it? [b]Mr. Cavill[/b]. Oh! Hush you. Besides, he hasn’t come into his inheritance yet. Oh, you know that’s not what I meant!”

entities.title: Busybody Martindale
entities.content: That [b]Mr. Anthony Martindale[/b] came to call last week, asking me the most personal questions – he said he was ‘looking into matters to help out a friend’ but if you ask me he’s just a fancy busybody.”

entities.title: Camille and Comtesse Pay Off
entities.content: “[b]Mrs. Camille Grey[/b] plays a proper lady, certainly, but she keeps strange company. I’ve seen money regularly exchanged between her and the [b]Comtesse de Lalande[/b]. Gambling or blackmail, I suspect.”

entities.title: Camille Grey Spy Extraordinaire
entities.content: [b]Mrs. Camille Grey[/b] is [i]the[/i] woman to go to when it comes to matters of intelligence or espionage. There are rumours, of course, that she’s an agent for the French… but that seems too obvious, no? Much more likely she’s out for herself…”

entities.title: Can't Marry Mary
entities.content: “@[[b]Lord Collin Davenport[/b]](person:9c8d167e-e8b4-42a4-9c23-3b2af707de87) has quite forbidden anyone from courting @[[b]Lady Mary Davenport[/b]](person:4b285322-9a2b-4ada-97b8-f0ea5f6e75a9), even though it’s been a few years since her debut. Rumor has it he dueled the last man that tried. If he weren’t from such a respectable family, one might worry there were inappropriate affections afoot.”

entities.title: Caperoy's Haughty Couture
entities.content: [b]Mrs. Lucy Caperoy[/b] has quite the sharp fashion sense, returned recently from France. It must be that modiste blood in her veins. I'd consider commissioning her for a bespoke piece, but she can be so uncivil...

entities.title: Captain Lewis' Recuperation
entities.content: [b]Captain Barnabas Thompson[/b] and [b]Miss Georgiana Thompson[/b] were so kind to let [b]Captain Silas Lewis[/b] recuperate from his wounds in their home. [b]Lt. Fletcher Montgomery[/b] just dumped him and left...

entities.title: Cassandra Vs. Augusta
entities.content: [b]Miss Cassandra Willoughby[/b] is the spitting image of her mother. [b]Mrs. Augusta Willougby[/b] is an enchanting woman, I daresay the orignal outshines her own daughter! Oh, don't tell Miss Willoughby I said that...

entities.title: Charlotte Collapses
entities.content: “[b]Miss Charlotte Waldock[/b] collapsed a few months ago, just outside of Madame Duvall’s dress shop. Her sister, [b]Miss Waldock[/b], was on hand, and quite hastily got her out of sight of the store, but I’m certain I saw it. Is she ill, poor thing, or was she drunk mid-afternoon? Who can tell, she’s such a wild, passionate little thing.”

entities.title: Chatsworth Chatting With Clark
entities.content: “[b]Miss Fanny Clark[/b] and the [b]Marquess of Chatsworth[/b] used to be seen publicly together. Can you imagine? What would he possibly see in her?”

entities.title: Chatsworth Compares Kestrel and Rosaline
entities.content: “The [b]Marquess of Chatsworth[/b]’s first love was that famous courtesan, what was her name again? [b]Kestrel[/b]’s mother. Stunningly beautiful. He was quite enchanted with her. I heard him speaking quite ill of [b]Miss Rosaline Paciello[/b]’s voice at the Opera House last week. Loud enough for just about anyone, including the director, to overhear. He compared her talent to Kestrel’s quite unfavourably… I wonder if there’s some rivalry there we don’t know about.”

entities.title: Chatsworth's Wife Count
entities.content: “The [b]Marquess of Chatsworth[/b] is on his third wife. Tragedies do happen, but isn’t it starting to be quite the string of bad luck?”

entities.title: Clark Protegee of Murray
entities.content: “That [b]Miss Murray[/b] — well, you know [b]Miss Clark[/b]? She’s made her a protegee, but shouldn’t Miss Murray worry about her prospects first? Even if Miss Clark needs the help.”

entities.title: Clark's Locket
entities.content: “Have you noticed [b]Miss Fanny Clark[/b] has a locket she never takes off? I wonder if she has a sweetheart.”

entities.title: Clean Camille Grey's Gloves of Red
entities.content: “Did you know [b]Mrs. Grey[/b] is on her third husband? The first one died under mysterious causes. They never proved anything either way, but I suspect her kid gloves aren’t entirely clean.”

entities.title: Club Middleton
entities.content: “I’ve heard [b]Mr. Middleton[/b] is planning on starting up a gentleman’s club. He’s no head for finance, though, I certainly wouldn’t invest... look at the state of his affairs. Look at his five unmarried daughters!”

entities.title: Coarse Knight
entities.content: “I simply cannot believe the manner in which that brute speaks. So coarse. I heard [b]Sir Rawdon Nash[/b] grew up in a cowpen, living right there amongst the animals. It’s a miracle he can speak at all. Come, let’s find more eloquent company.”

entities.title: Cold Collin Towards Miss Willoughby
entities.content: “Exacting, precise, and cold. [b]Miss Willoughby[/b] is exactly the type of woman who makes for a miserable husband, despite her great beauty. No wonder [b]Lord Collin Davenport[/b] hasn’t proposed to her, despite how long they’ve known each other.”

entities.title: Colonel Montgomery Shot His Shot
entities.content: “[b]Colonel Montgomery[/b] duelled a man years ago over a woman’s honour, I heard. It’s all been quite hush hush, but it fatally wounded [b]Mrs. Lewis[/b]'. Lewis’ husband, Mr. Lewis. He died quite suddenly and left her a widow, poor thing. Unsurprising, the Colonel is a remarkable shot, always has been.”

entities.title: Company Losses for Harringtons
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Harrington[/b] is back in the country from India. I wonder if that’s to do with his family’s fortunes. I heard there was a great storm that way last season, lost a ton of shipments myself…”

entities.title: Constables and Colonel Montgomery
entities.content: “Came down to be interviewed by a constable last week. Yes, @[[b]Colonel Charles Montgomery[/b]](person:0b202c8a-f485-423d-978f-be0796ebb284) of all people. Has he been the victim of some crime or another? Or the perpetrator?”

entities.title: Constables Want Waldocks
entities.content: “A constable visited the [b]Waldock[/b] girls the other night. I wonder whatever for. Was there some trouble?”

entities.title: Crass Cross
entities.content: “What a harrowing debut [b]Miss Bridget Cross[/b] had. She’s been out for several years now, hasn’t she? New money is always crass and she’s no exception. I wouldn’t let my young ladies fraternize with her.”

entities.title: Cross Disappointment
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Thomas Cross[/b] has long been a disappointment to his father, [b]Sir Miles[/b]. The wealthy industrialist no doubt expected his son to follow in his footsteps, but the boy seems to have no head for business whatsoever. I heard he wants to enter the clergy.”

entities.title: Cross My Clark
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Thomas Cross[/b] danced at least twice with [b]Miss Clark[/b] at the last ball, and were later seen having quite a tense conversation. Is there more going on there than meets the eye?”

entities.title: Cross My Heart
entities.content: “[b]Sir Miles Cross[/b] lost his wife a number of years ago. He seems heartless, but the two of them worked their way up from nothing and built an empire. She wasn’t just his wife, she was his partner. He hasn’t ever been the same since. I imagine it pains him, all the young ladies thrown at him for his wealth.”

entities.title: Crossed Over
entities.content: “[b]Miss Bridget Cross[/b] is the more disappointing of the Cross girls. [b]Sir Miles[/b] may be the richest man in England, but he certainly couldn’t buy his daughter grace or charm. His eldest, now she was a force to be reckoned with. She married, however, quite well. She’s [b]Mrs, Pembroke[/b] now. [b]Mr. Pembroke[/b] stands to be a Viscount after his father. Hm, yes. Now what was I saying? Yes, Miss Cross... quite forgettable, poor thing…”

entities.title: Death of Doctor's Sister
entities.content: “I overheard there was some mysterious business about that [b]Doctor Hugh Wentworth[/b]. His sister, Mary Ann, died in India. They said it was cholera, but, well. You know how people talk.”

entities.title: Diamond of Devonshire
entities.content: “[b]Miss Spencer[/b], the incomparable debutante of the season, has declared quite publicly her intentions to be courted by [b]Duke of Devonshire[/b]. Will he answer the call, do you think?”

entities.title: Disappearing Mary Davenport
entities.content: “[b]Lady Mary Davenport[/b] always disappears into the shadows, doesn’t she? Often overlooked for her older brother, the heir. But she’s a kind, thoughtful young woman. I only wish they’d let her out of the house now and again... Instead of keeping her locked up like a prisoner.”

entities.title: Doctor Anne Middleton
entities.content: “[b]Miss Anne Middleton[/b] used to assist [b]Mr. Hastings[/b]’ father, the doctor around Oldmeadow. She helped deliver my youngest girl, bless her heart. Miss Anne is a mighty serious, intense thing- but she worked well and hard while the old man was still alive, rest his soul.”

entities.title: Doctor's Hair Tonic
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Edwin Murray[/b] says that [b]Doctor Wentworth[/b] makes him this incredible Bekara Tonic that has grown him an entirely new head of hair! He swears by the stuff, apparently, it's an absolute miracle. I must find some, do you think he'll sell it to me?”

entities.title: Dostov Deportation
entities.content: “I heard that [b]Baroness Katarina Dostov[/b]'s husband might be recalled home any day now. No, of course it isn't a shame, can you really trust a Russian?”

entities.title: Duke At Last
entities.content: “The [b]Duke of Devonshire[/b] is clearly the wealthiest and most powerful man here, but he hasn’t been seen around London since he went to school. He rarely visited before, it’s so exciting to finally meet him.”

entities.title: Duke or Marquess?
entities.content: “Doesn’t the [b]Duke of Devonshire[/b] look suspiciously like [b]Marquess of Chatsworth[/b]? That man’s father’s second wife (or was it his third?) disappeared under mysterious circumstances afterall… Marianne Wharton was her name.”

entities.title: Duke Swap
entities.content: Is in disguise as his friend, Alexander Hale

entities.title: Dulled Diamond Wetherlocke
entities.content: [b]Mrs. Helena Wetherlocke[/b] was once a diamond of the season. It's a shame her husband's death appears to have tarnished her luster.

entities.title: Dulling Miss Sharpe's Shine
entities.content: “Never before have I seen such lost promise as [b]Miss Hester Sharpe[/b]. She was named the diamond of the season when she debuted, was engaged briefly to- who was it again? [b]Mr. Middleton[/b], I think? And then all that scandal, her sister’s bastard daughter and all... Society has almost forgotten but I haven’t.”

entities.title: Elster and Miss Wickham
entities.content: I once saw the dandy [b]Elster[/b] and [b]Miss Isabella Wickham[/b] whispering rather closely. They barely ever acknowledge eachother in public! Curious, curious...

entities.title: Elster's Grand Tour
entities.content: [b]Mr. Timothy Harrington[/b] and [b]Major James Osbourne[/b] received a private tour of Berlin, courtesy of [b]Elster[/b]. I'm sure they saw all kinds of unsavory delights whilst in their company.

entities.title: Emma's Non-Debut
entities.content: “An odd girl, [b]Miss Emma Waldock[/b] . She broke her arm just after her debut this year, poor thing. But all she knows how to talk about is rocks and dirt. Incredibly boring.”

entities.title: Fairweather Tears for Miss Spencer
entities.content: “[b]Miss Spencer[/b] left the Fairweather Ball in tears the night of her debut. What on earth happened to her?”

entities.title: Fast Dash for Miss Nash
entities.content: “There’s an incredible horse coming into the races soon. He’s owned by [b]Miss Elizabeth Nash[/b] and he’s the fastest damned thing I have ever seen. If he doesn’t win a dozen races next year I’ll eat my hat.”

entities.title: Father-Duke Split
entities.content: “Familial bliss has never been the way of things with the [b]Duke of Devonshire[/b] and his late father. I doubt he shed a tear over the old man. Now that he’s back from touring Europe, he’s the eligble young man all these ladies will want to meet...”

entities.title: Fencing Georgiana
entities.content: “I heard [b]Miss Thompson[/b] was taught to fence alongside her brother.”

entities.title: Fletcher and The Flying Rose
entities.content: That [b]Lt. Fletcher Montgomery[/b] is a scary man indeed, if a man and not a monster he truly be. Sunk a retreating ship to smithereens, all for naught! Those pirates have been harrying us ever since!

entities.title: Frivolous Phoebe Middleton
entities.content: “[b]Miss Phoebe Middleton[/b] has been running quite wild, trying to coax some of the unmarried young women into some frivolity. I certainly hope she pulls it off. After all, a young woman needs companions her own age.”

entities.title: Geoffrey and Mother
entities.content: “I thought that [b]Mr. Geoffrey Priestly[/b] was always closer to his father than his mother, [b]Lady Francesca Priestly[/b]. I wonder at how often they’re together this Season...he's never seemed over-fond of her. Of course, that feeling seems mutual, now I think of it.”

entities.title: Geoffrey Priestly Knows Wether Locked
entities.content: “I heard [b]Mr. Geoffrey Priestly[/b] was with Mr. Wetherlocke the night he died...Neither of them are the sort to risk their lives, and he'd told [i]me[/i] he was going to somewhere quite unsavory that evening. I wonder what really happened?”

entities.title: Ghostly Anora
entities.content: That wisp of a girl, [b]Lady Anora Cherrington[/b], is an odd duck. I heard she saw a [i]ghost[/i] once, preposterous!

entities.title: Glover Avoids Anora
entities.content: [b]Lady Anora Cherrington[/b] is the unfortunate ward of that debaucherous [b]Lord Jeremy Glover[/b]. Apparently, he can barely stand to be in the same room with her...avoids her like the plague!

entities.title: Good for the Governess Hastings
entities.content: “All alone in that big, empty house with a rich, handsome, widowed Lord... I’m not saying [b]Miss Hastings[/b] has acted improperly, mind you, but we’ve all noticed the way [b]Lord Montague[/b] looks at her. Who could blame her if she had?”

entities.title: Grand Tour For Bennington and Radcliffe
entities.content: “The well-traveled [b]Miss Bennington[/b] just returned from the continent with [b]Miss Rebecca Radcliffe[/b]. They’ve been on tour for nearly twenty years- I wonder what on earth brought them back. Wealthy spinstresses traveling together, one does ask questions...”

entities.title: Grey and Martindale's Missed Connection
entities.content: [b]Mrs. Camille Grey[/b] and [b]Mr. Anthony Martindale[/b] often shared the dance floor in their youth...before she was married of course. I always always thought they looked so well togehter, what a shame.

entities.title: Grey Trading Lalande
entities.content: “I heard the [b]Comtesse de Lalande[/b] once helped someone with a 'little problem'- she knows all sorts of people, good and bad. I've seen her and [b]Mrs. Camille Grey[/b] trading packets and money before... one always wonders what happened to Mrs. Grey's first husband... and what will happen to her current one....”

entities.title: Hale But Cross
entities.content: “There was some bitter falling out between [b]Sir Miles Cross[/b] and his old partner, Mr. James Hale. The Hale family made some bad business dealings and got bought out years back, now the Cross family is wealthy as sin, and [b]Mr. Alexander Hale[/b] can barely afford his solicitor’s apprenticeship. It must chafe. But Sir Miles has never been a generous man, that's for sure and certain.”

entities.title: Hale's Mother
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Hale[/b]’s mother was higher class than she had any right to be, marrying Mr. James Hale. Rumor has it she used to be married to the [b]Marquess of Chatsworth[/b]’s father. Would that make them half-brothers?”

entities.title: Hale's Proscribed Readings
entities.content: “I’ve heard [b]Mr. Alexander Hale[/b] has been employed printing seditious pamphlets and distributing them among the working poor. Absurd passtime for someone training to be a solicitor, no one will ever hire him with such black marks on his character.”

entities.title: Hard as Steele
entities.content: “[b]Miss Sophia Steele[/b] is a rough young woman; she attended the Lowood School for Girl’s. Wasn’t [b]Lord Jeremy Glover[/b]’s uncle murdered there? That is how he got his title, after all.”

entities.title: Harrington Has Pineapples
entities.content: “A whole parcel of pineapples, I heard. No, not possible. Those are so rare most people have never tried one. Well, [b]Mr. Harrington[/b] has at least one with him, and he plans on having a tasting during this outing… what a sensuous affair!”

entities.title: Harrington Jilting Spencer for a Duke
entities.content: “I heard [b]Miss Harrington[/b] is trying to marry above her station and has her eyes on the [b]Duke of Devonshire[/b]. That would show her pompous fiancé, [b]Lt. Spencer[/b]. He’s never loved that girl the way she deserves.”

entities.title: Harrington's Broken Heart
entities.content: “I’ve heard he has a wife in India that he never talks about, that [b]Mr. Harrington[/b]. Either that, or [b]Miss Carroway[/b] broke his heart years ago by refusing to marry him and he’s been too choked up about it to consider love again.”

entities.title: Hartford and the Spencers
entities.content: “For nearly a year, [b]Hartford[/b] was a constant visitor at Prewitt House, the Spencer’s family home. Were they visiting [b]Miss Spencer[/b] well into the night or her brother...?”

entities.title: Hartford Cracking Pembroke
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Pembroke[/b] was asking me questions the other day about [b]Hartford[/b]. What on earth kind of business those two might have together, I couldn’t begin to say. A proper future Viscount and a dandy who likes to spout poetry? Maybe he’s finally decided to take up a hobby that isn’t balancing his chequebook.”

entities.title: Hartford Fits Like a Glove(r)
entities.content: “[b]Hartford[/b] and [b]Lord Glover[/b] used to be bosom companions, always at each other’s sides and functions, gossiping and being seen at White’s together. There was some disastrous falling out; however, and now they rarely attend the same functions.”

entities.title: Hartford For Hire
entities.content: “[b]Hartford[/b] is [i]the[/i] portrait artist to hire. No one, simply no one, has their eye.”

entities.title: Hartford Transforms Miss Spencer
entities.content: “[b]Miss Spencer[/b] was quite transformed by the dandy [b]Hartford[/b]. They taught her how to dress, how to dance, the lot of it. She was such an awkward, nervous little thing last year, now look at her! The Incomparable of the season, likely to be married to the [b]Duke of Devonshire[/b] of Devonshire himself…”

entities.title: Hastings Needed Haste
entities.content: “No, he missed his exam. Missed his Naval Lieutenant’s Exam. I don’t mean failed it, I mean failed to show up for it! Yes, [b]Mr. Hastings[/b]. God knows what he’ll do now, they’ll never make him an officer...”

entities.title: Hastings' Thompson Rescue
entities.content: “I heard [b]Mr. William Hastings[/b] saved [b]Captain Barnabas Thompson[/b]'s life years ago at sea. There was a great storm and the Captain's ship was about to be scuttled - but Mr. Hastings dove off the Navy ship he was serving on to rescue him.”

entities.title: Honorable Mr. Middleton
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Middleton[/b] plays the curmudgeon, but in truth he’s one of the kindest men I know. Right honorable, him. And all that, even burdened with all those daughters…”

entities.title: Jos and Julia
entities.content: “I heard that [b]Mr. Jos Pembroke[/b] is courting [b]Miss Julia Waldock[/b]. That won't end well, mark my words. She’s a nice enough girl, but absolutely no dowry to speak of. And with his older brother, Mr. Pembroke, marrying so poorly... it relies on Mr. Jos to snag himself at least a Countess.”

entities.title: Jos Carroway Seeds
entities.content: “[b]Miss Violet Carraway[/b] has been spending a considerable amount of time with [b]Mr. Jos Pembroke[/b]. I know it’s not my place, and she is your cousin, but perhaps you should speak to her chaperone, and elder sister, [b]Mrs. Camille Grey[/b]? We all know you’ve been looking into a marriage to settle Mr. Jos and I know you wouldn’t want his ways to ruin a young lady’s reputation.”

entities.title: Jos' Letters
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Jos Pembroke[/b] sends dozens of letters but I haven’t the faintest idea who he is writing. They’re all sent out in secret, must be, since they’re never in the family’s mail... or perhaps he’s writing a novel...”

entities.title: Kestrel Gives Society the Bird
entities.content: “I’ve never seen someone so flippant about society as [b]Kestrel[/b]. She sword fights, she sings opera, and she’s a menace to society. The only reason she’s tolerated at all and not thrown into some prison for indecency is because she has some wealthy protector. I heard her father might be the Prince Regent himself…”

entities.title: Kestrel Got Thee From a Nunnery
entities.content: “[b]Kestrel[/b] broke her way into a nunnery and escaped with a young sister on horseback after fighting off that woman’s father with a sword. They’d told him he couldn’t keep them apart, and apparently they’d meant it. I wonder what happened to the nun...”

entities.title: Kid Glover for the Zoo
entities.content: “[b]Lord Glover[/b] keeps a whole menagerie at his country estate, fills the building beautiful things. Wild beasts, preening peacocks, ravenous wolves, proud bucks, and demurring kittens... and then there are the actual animals, haha...”

entities.title: Knightley in Love
entities.content: “[b]Knightley[/b] has it bad. Oh, no, don’t make that face. They are absolutely in love for the first time in their life. I wonder if they’ve finally met their match. If so, I wonder what a terror she must be…”

entities.title: Knightley Sighs for Love
entities.content: “@[[b]Knightley[/b]](person:032dc6b7-44ea-49dc-a76b-a2e0b59de07a) is in love. Just look at the way they sigh, the idle looks they give. I wonder what young woman has caught their attentions. And what they intend to do to win her...”

entities.title: Knightley Sighs for Love
entities.content: “[b]Knightley[/b] is in love. Just look at the way they sigh, the idle looks they give. I wonder what young woman has caught their attentions. And what they intend to do to win her...”

entities.title: Knightley V Hartford
entities.content: “[b]Knightley[/b] and [b]Hartford[/b] are sworn enemies. Both the pinnacle of fashion and poise and constantly at each other’s throats. Something to do with a difference in philosophy, the meaning of love, or how to tie a cravat, I don’t know. I heard that once [b]Peregrine[/b] had to pull them off of each other before it came to blows or an actual duel.”

entities.title: Knightley's a Right Bastard
entities.content: “An insufferable bastard. He's ruined her, I tell you, just ruined her. And for what? They would crush a rose just to watch the petal fall, these people- no, [b]Knightley[/b], the dandy. Of course they've covered their tracks so they'll never see - shh, someone's coming!”

entities.title: Lady Mary of the Foundlings
entities.content: “I heard [b]Lady Mary Davenport[/b] has been supporting the building of a Foundling Hospital. What possible reason would a young woman have to even know about children born out of wedlock, let alone to spend her time writing to solicit support? It’s rather telling, isn’t it. And here she comes of such good stock, too.”

entities.title: Lady Nash's Militia Collection
entities.content: “[b]Lady Nash[/b] has been seen inappropriately about the Ton with [b]Lt. Foxsmith [i]and[/i] Major Radcliffe.[/b] At this point she seems to be quite collecting young men of the militia, at least a decade younger than herself…”

entities.title: Lady Priestly - Arbiter of Taste
entities.content: “[b]Lady Priestly[/b] defines fashions as they are. She is at the forefront of any new look, and every lady in society looks to her to define what is correct. You would be a fool not to listen to her advice, should she ever look your way. She’s been friends with [b]Lady Davenport[/b] for years and keeps them both at the forefront of society.”

entities.title: Lady Priestly's Marriage
entities.content: “@[[b]Lady Francesca Priestly[/b]](person:17492303-aecb-4f1b-a6a6-19a31b5d50e9) and her husband, @[Lord Frederick Priestly](person:c5d30415-be40-4b06-9f94-05c9a0644e7c), have been estranged these last twenty years. He’s a philanderer, committed adultery with half the Ton. He left for the country house and hasn’t been seen in London since. Well, I certainly wouldn’t be anywhere Lady Priestly didn’t want me, that’s for certain. That woman is a force.”

entities.title: Lady Priestly's Marriage
entities.content: “[b]Lady Francesca Priestly[/b] and her husband, Lord Frederick Priestly, have been estranged these last twenty years. He’s a philanderer, committed adultery with half the Ton. He left for the country house and hasn’t been seen in London since. Well, I certainly wouldn’t be anywhere Lady Priestly didn’t want me, that’s for certain. That woman is a force.”

entities.title: Lalande and Viscount Pembroke
entities.content: “The [b]Comtesse de Lalande[/b] has long been a, ahem, visitor of the Viscount - no, no. [b]Mr. Pembroke[/b]’s father. Yes, with his mother right there in the house. Madame de Lalande is hardly older than Mr. Pembroke, and a servant told me they’ve often been at odds in their way. He attempted to banish her from the house once, as a younger man, but it seems it was unsuccessful. I wouldn’t want a woman like her as my enemy, anyway. I heard that diamond necklace alone cost more than a carriage.”

entities.title: Lalande Escaped the Guillotine
entities.content: “The [b]Comtesse de Lalande[/b] fled the Revolution, but her husband didn’t make it. If what is said about her is believed to be true, I would wonder if she didn’t have a hand in that chain of events somehow...”

entities.title: Lalande Living the Dream
entities.content: “Widowed and rich? The [b]Comtesse de Lalande[/b] really won out on the rest of us, didn’t she. Lost her husband during the Reign of Terror in France. I heard the Comte was nearly twice her age.”

entities.title: Laura Between Cavill and Willoughby
entities.content: “[b]Miss Laura Middleton[/b] has been entertaining both [b]Mr. Martin Cavill[/b] and [b]Mr. Bruce Willoughby[/b]. She’ll need to pick one eventually, the tease.”

entities.title: Laurie Fiddled and Prewitt House Burned
entities.content: “@[[b]Major Radcliffe[/b]](person:adaab4af-763b-4b42-8c38-2d98f70245ad), @[[b]Lt. Foxsmith[/b]](person:71777950-6f6e-41ce-896c-ce17f10a5752), @[[b]Lt. Spencer[/b]](person:122186c2-774a-43a4-9c26-ba067474ca78), and @[[b]Major Osbourne[/b]](person:8fe90b6d-2b6d-4c71-bbc5-a616ebe5fbea) are inseparable. I heard they were all drunk out of their heads the night that Prewitt House, where the Spencer family lives, burned, and that Lt. Foxsmith saved their lives... and @[[b]Miss Spencer[/b]](person:0fb14ae9-02eb-41ff-bd68-02036ee7b950)’s, too!”

entities.title: Laurie Fiddled and Prewitt House Burned
entities.content: “[b]Major Radcliffe[/b], [b]Lt. Foxsmith[/b], [b]Lt. Spencer[/b], and [b]Major Osbourne[/b] are inseparable. I heard they were all drunk out of their heads the night that Prewitt House, where the Spencer family lives, burned, and that Lt. Foxsmith saved their lives... and [b]Miss Spencer[/b]’s, too!”

entities.title: Lieutenant Montgomery has it in for Elster
entities.content: “I heard that [b]Lieutenant Fletcher Montgomery's[/b] reputation was entirely ruined by Lady Chatterbox and that cartoonist dandy, [b]Elster[/b]. Lady Chatterbox may be anonymous, but I'm surprised that the lieutenant hasn't challenged Elster to a duel!”

entities.title: Lizzy Nash, Horse Girl
entities.content: “[b]Miss Elizabeth Nash[/b] rode a horse in proper jockey form when she ran up to my farmhouse last week. Apparently my sheep had all gotten loose, but she’d wrangled them up for me on horseback like it was nothing! I swear, the two of them together, she and that horse were two parts of the same whole. Both made for the moors, both manes tugging in the wind. Fast as the devil, that beast. Hah- no, I don’t know if I mean the horse or the girl.”

entities.title: Lonely Mrs. Lewis
entities.content: “Mr. Lewis, [b]Mrs. Lewis[/b]’ late husband was killed tragically in an illegal duel last year. She’s now widowed, poor thing, long before she had a family. All that’s left to her is one younger sister.”

entities.title: Lowood and Glover
entities.content: “Isn’t it scandalous? Some say [b]Lord Glover[/b]’s uncle, the previous Earl of Glover, was murdered and that it wasn’t an accident at all. At that terrible school, the Lowood School for Girls.”

entities.title: Lt. Foxsmith and Lady Nash
entities.content: “A penchant for women much older, so I heard. There was a passionate affair with [b]Lady Margaret Nash[/b] a while ago. Mmm, that very same [b]Lt. Jack Foxsmith[/b]. It would be scandalous, of course, but she’s conveniently widowed now...”

entities.title: Lying Miss Malet
entities.content: “A rotten liar, that [b]Miss Penelope Malet[/b]. Always making up fictions, particularly about her classmates or girls she’s jealous of. I heard she reduced a girl to absolute tears with a cruel story about her and that the retelling of the story became so prevalent the girl needed to move to the Cottswolds!”

entities.title: Major Gambler
entities.content: “[b]Major Lawrence Radcliffe[/b] is a rake and a gambler; he wins more than he loses. For now.”

entities.title: Malet's Ambitions for Marriage
entities.content: “[b]Miss Malet[/b], [b]Lady Davenport[/b]’s lady’s companion, has aims far above her station. That wild-eyed young woman is inclined to wild flights of fancy and has been seen flirting with [b]Sir Lucian[/b]. Any marriage would be a step up for her, seeing as she is a penniless orphan.”

entities.title: Many, Many Middletons
entities.content: “Never did I see a family so absurd as [b]the Middletons[/b]. They’re practically destitute, they’ve five daughters all of an age and not a one yet married. It’s incredible they keep being invited to polite society gatherings.”

entities.title: Marquess Moves on Tatiana
entities.content: “Did you hear [b]Lady Tatiana[/b] screaming at that ball a few weeks ago? I heard it was a way to make it seem like she hadn't been compromised by that [b]Marquess[/b]. But we have only her and her mother's word that nothing more happened in that hallway just outside the festivities, but we all know the things they said about those empresses of Russia...”

entities.title: Martindale Gentleman Detective
entities.content: That [b]Mr. Anthony Martindale[/b] has been dabbling somewhat as a detective. Nothing too serious: a misplaced letter here, a missing friend there...but who's to say that he can't unearth something bigger? He's quite talented!

entities.title: Martindepressed to Martindandy
entities.content: “I heard Captain– I mean [b]Mr. Anthony Martindale[/b] came back from Flanders a broken man, and was a recluse for three years after. You’d never know it now, looking at the sharp fashion on him…”

entities.title: Middleton's Philosophy Club
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Middleton[/b] has the most incredible idea to start a gentleman’s club for debating philosophy rather than simply drinking and gambling. What? Of course, I like drinking and gambling, but I like philosophy just as well. Don’t you want to better yourself over a fine meal and drinks?”

entities.title: Middleton's Sharpeness
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Middleton[/b] used to have a flirtation with [b]Miss Hester Sharpe[/b]. They’ve not seen each other in many years. But considering she never married and her reputation was sullied by her sister [b]Miss Mildred Sharpe[/b]’s illicit affair and disappearance, you must wonder if she was heartbroken afterwards...”

entities.title: Middletons Mooning Over Hastings
entities.content: “[b]Miss Anne Middleton[/b] and her sister [b]Miss Alice[/b] have both been mooning over [b]Mr. William Hastings[/b] since they were children. Eventually he’ll have to marry one of them, as he certainly can’t have both sisters…”

entities.title: Mining Nash
entities.content: “I heard [b]Sir Rawdon Nash[/b] has been sitting in on mining business meetings with [b]Lady Nash[/b], but never says anything. I wonder if he has any idea what she's up to.”

entities.title: Miss Anne Middleton Missed Out
entities.content: “One might almost feel sorry for [b]Miss Anne Middleton[/b]. She’s brilliant, determined, and inventive. She can be too direct, too brusque. All of those are poor traits in a young girl. More’s the pity she wasn’t born a boy. The Middletons could have used a son.”

entities.title: Miss Carroway's Gifts
entities.content: “I’ve seen [b]Miss Violet Carroway[/b] buying gifts she can’t hardly afford now and again, and slipping off at all odd hours of the night. What on earth could she possibly be up to?”

entities.title: Miss Murray's Disinterest in Marriage
entities.content: “It’s difficult not to love [b]Miss Murray[/b], she’s such a darling girl. She takes care of her mother now that her father is dead and her elder sister is married, and seems much more interested in matchmaking for other people than for herself as suits a lady her age.”

entities.title: Miss Nash's Suitors
entities.content: “[b]Miss Nash[/b] has [b]Mr. Tournelle[/b] and [b]Sir Rawdon Nash[/b] absolutely mad about her – but she’s scornful of their courtships. It would certainly tie things up neatly, for her to marry the man that inherited her family’s home. No, no. Sir Rawdon. Not that penniless bastard Tournelle.”

entities.title: Miss Paciello's Accidents
entities.content: “[b]Miss Rosaline Paciello[/b], the opera singer, has had a series of nearly fatal accidents at the opera house. Completely unheard of, what horrible safety methods are they using there. No wonder the place is to be sold off…”

entities.title: Miss Phoebe's Fate
entities.content: “[b]Miss Phoebe Middleton[/b] is wildly precocious. She’s going to get into trouble one of these days, mark my word. That is, if she hasn’t already.”

entities.title: Miss Radcliffe's Unusual Business
entities.content: “[b]Miss Radcliffe[/b] has had strange, strange business dealings since she returned from the continent. She hasn’t been home in nearly twenty years, and suddenly within days of arriving - look here. She bought several properties, all about town. Fine homes, yes, but also this - a tavern and a gambling den? A physicians? A work house? And investing in jewels, fine art, various artifacts and then selling them mere months later... what is she on about?”

entities.title: Miss Thompson's Asset Commentary
entities.content: “No restraint whatsoever. [b]Miss Thompson[/b] made a very inappropriate comment to my daughter about her features within my hearing. I was shocked beyond belief, and further shocked to find my own daughter giggling at the comment!”

entities.title: Mistress Comtess
entities.content: “The [b]Comtesse de Lalande[/b] used to be the mistress of [b]Lord Pembroke[/b], [b]Mr. Arthur[/b] and [b]Mr. Joseph Pembroke[/b]’s father. And was quite publicly disliked by Mr. Pembroke. It was very nearly a scandal.”

entities.title: Montague Accidentally Inherits
entities.content: “Mr. Carver Montague, [b]Lord Montague[/b]’s older brother, died in a horrible accident that left him quite disfigured. Lord Montague didn’t shed a tear. Half makes me wonder if he was responsible for it. After all, it did lead to his inheriting... and marrying his brother’s fiance... or was it the fiance’s sister?”

entities.title: Montague and the Governess
entities.content: “[b]Lord Montague[/b] has strange goings-on at his ancestral home. I’ve heard tell his first wife didn’t actually die, but instead she went quite mad after the loss of a child. Now she’s locked up in his attic and he’s parading about with his little governess, [b]Miss Hastings[/b]...”

entities.title: Montgomery A Murderer
entities.content: “[b]Mrs. Lewis[/b] had a husband, [b]Mr. Byron Lewis[/b], who died quite terribly two years ago. They tried to play it off as a fever, but I spoke to that doctor - it was an injury from a sword, you mark my word. Dueling illegally. By all suspicions, it was a duel with the honourable [b]Colonel Montgomery[/b]. Whatever he did to offend that man’s honour, I can’t imagine...”

entities.title: Montgomery Mongrels
entities.content: Those Montgomery Mongrels, [b]Lieutenant Fletcher Montgomery[/b] & [b]Mrs. Helena Wetherlocke[/b], certainly do find themselves in a scandalous fog, don't they? Corpses abound, it must come from their lowly mother's bloodline.

entities.title: Mr. Martin Montgomery?
entities.content: “I suspect [b]Colonel Charles Montgomery[/b] is actually [b]Mr. Cavill[/b]’s father. I overheard [b]Mrs. Whitney Huxley[/b] and the Colonel talking about his schooling, and he promised to send more funds for the boy. Who pays for a boy’s schooling if they aren’t that boy’s father?”

entities.title: Mr. Murray's Sharpe Disappearance
entities.content: “Long ago, @[[b]Mr. Edwin Murray[/b]](person:e401c266-3f1a-43e2-874d-f5f2e026e04d) disappeared into the country for several months. It would have predated @[[b]Miss Amelia Murray[/b]](person:81818c4e-8ba3-4316-aa44-a49a65d8df06)’s birth considerably, but I always wonder... @[Miss Mildred Sharpe](person:abfead6a-b240-4f08-aeec-ececcc46a217) went missing around that time as well, didn’t she? Those two were always bosom companions.”

entities.title: Mrs. Caperoy Vs. Miss Willoughby
entities.content: I've never seen a cattier pair than [b]Miss Cassandra Willoughby[/b] and [b]Mrs. Lucy Caperoy[/b]. They way they constantly hiss at each other, why it makes even my hackles raise.

entities.title: Mrs. Lewis' Business Acumen
entities.content: “[b]Mrs. Esther Lewis[/b] has kept herself and her business in fine shape since her husband’s unfortunate passing. He was lost in a duel, so I heard, but it’s been kept quite hushed. She’s a brilliant woman, but has her younger sister [b]Miss Crane[/b] to look after.”

entities.title: Murray's Mentees
entities.content: [b]@[Miss Amelia Murray](person:81818c4e-8ba3-4316-aa44-a49a65d8df06)[/b] has two young proteges, [b]@[Miss Bridget Cross](person:0ff4f577-7cc0-45f2-97b0-f0311b8a3ab1)[/b] and [b]@[Miss Fanny Clark](person:6021bf56-fa01-4b78-acc3-faca295882fb)[/b] that she is trying to get married off. Difficult matches - Miss Cross is so plain and uncomfortable, even with her fortune. And Miss Clark is poor, so even her notable good looks might not win her a husband worth having...

entities.title: Opera Troubles
entities.content: “The Grand Opera House is on its last sixpence. Where does it end up next?”

entities.title: Ornery Anne
entities.content: “The dour [b]Miss Anne Middleton[/b] is like a little storm cloud entering any event. I saw her and [b]Major Osbourne[/b] dance at the last ball, and he stepped on both her feet. The girl gave him a glare that would bore a hole right through his temple, if she had the power.”

entities.title: Overambitious Miss Wickham
entities.content: “She’ll never amount to much, [b]Miss Wickham[/b]. A grabbing, clawing little thing if I ever saw one. She intends to marry well and doesn’t give a damn how she does it.”

entities.title: Papa of Pippa's Kid
entities.content: “[b]Lt. Spencer[/b] and [b]Mrs. Pembroke[/b] had a whirlwind courtship last year. And now, hardly months later, she’s married to [b]Mr. Pembroke[/b] and they’ve already had an heir? I sense scandal afoot.”

entities.title: Partridge in Spencer's Tree
entities.content: “[b]The Spencers[/b] used to own Depardieu’s last painting, [i]The Partridge[/i]. Burned in that great fire at their house a few years back, though dinnit? Well I heard tell it’s going up for sale. Howsat, if it burned?”

entities.title: Pembroke's Racket in the Attic
entities.content: “A servant told me that [b]Mrs. Pembroke[/b] has quite filled the attic of Hartfield with all matter of machinery, and that an infernal racket emanates from it all day and night. Quite improper, what with her newborn son to think about.”

entities.title: Penniless Doctor Hastings
entities.content: “[b]Miss Hastings[/b]’ father was a doctor, but he left his children penniless. Too generous, took too many calls for nothing but wine and thanks. Some well-off cousin or another paid for her to go to Lowood but she was practically a servant there. Too bad, waste of a pretty girl becoming a governess but there’s nothing for it, with no family except her brother [b]Mr. Hastings[/b] to speak of.”

entities.title: Peregrine One Requesting Landing
entities.content: “[b]Peregrine[/b] invented being a dandy. There was no where he wasn’t seen or talked about. But now, in their later years, they are starting to fade from the public opinion. I wonder what they’ll busy themself with now?”

entities.title: Peregrine Perches in Priestly's Bush
entities.content: “[b]Peregrine[/b] and [b]Lady Priestly[/b] have been having an affair for years that they’ve kept under wraps. They blackmailed her husband, Lord Priestly, into silence while they made him a cuckold.”

entities.title: Poor Emma Waldock
entities.content: “[b]Miss Emma Waldock[/b] has spent several summers down by the sea, unearthing strange rocks and digging deep holes in the sand. No one has the foggiest idea why. Poor girl, to lose her parents so young and have to rely on the charity of others.”

entities.title: Poor Miss Middleton
entities.content: “If the Middletons weren’t so poorly managed, their eldest daughter [b]Miss Laura Middleton[/b] might have been married by now. She’s pretty enough. Shame about the family.”

entities.title: Printing Miss Carroway
entities.content: “There are a series of odd shops, down in Printer’s Row. I’ve seen [b]Miss Carroway[/b] there now and again, I’m certain it's her. Socializing with ruffians. I wonder if she’s taken a lover.”

entities.title: Radcliffe likes Miss Murray
entities.content: “@[[b]Major Radcliffe[/b]](person:adaab4af-763b-4b42-8c38-2d98f70245ad) frequently calls at @[[b]Miss Murray’s[/b]](person:81818c4e-8ba3-4316-aa44-a49a65d8df06) home. He may be more interested in her than [b]Miss Murray[/b] is aware.”

entities.title: Radcliffe the Hunter
entities.content: “[b]Mr. John Radcliffe[/b] was, in his day, quite the avid hunter. Best shot I ever saw, impossibly steady. He used to take, on his jaunts, several dozen braces of pheasants (a brace is a pair, cock and hen) which was exceptional.”

entities.title: Radcliffe's Back
entities.content: “[b]Miss Radcliffe[/b] hasn’t been seen in nearly twenty years. Why has she suddenly shown back up now?”

entities.title: Rakish Duke
entities.content: “The [b]Duke of Devonshire[/b] is quite the rake. I've heard he's had a whole string of lovers and refuses to be tied down to any one woman. It would be a right scandal were it anyone else, but you know the nobility...”

entities.title: Reverend Social Climbing
entities.content: “He may be a reverend, yes, but he’s far from humble. [b]Reverend Robert Lewis[/b] has bounced from one wealthy friend to another for years and shows no signs of stopping. [b]Lord Montague[/b] was first, now on to bigger and better things...”

entities.title: Revolutionary Mr. Hale
entities.content: “A young revolutionary, that [b]Mr. Hale[/b]. I read a pamphlet he wrote about the rights of the workers a few months ago on the green at Cambridge. I’ve never read such fire before.”

entities.title: Rude Ruth
entities.content: “[b]Mrs. Ruth Middleton[/b] is one of the most uncouth women I’ve ever met. I once saw her break down into absolute hysteria over a misplaced pack of rump roast. Rump roast!”

entities.title: Russian Around
entities.content: “That [b]Baroness Katarina Dostov[/b] is utterly shameless. I heard she used her husband's carriage to go to the opera with [b]Mr. Geoffrey Priestly[/b]. Some men will put up with anything!”

entities.title: Sarah With A Needle
entities.content: “[b]Miss Crane[/b] does the most beautiful needlepoint. There is something to be said for a woman with such skills.”

entities.title: Sarah's Golden Needle
entities.content: “[b]Miss Sarah Crane[/b] has the most impressive needle point I’ve ever seen. Truly her work is worth its weight in gold.”

entities.title: Scandalous Kestrel
entities.content: “[b]Kestrel[/b] is married to some Spanish swordsmaster, but she carries on with all sorts of women and men alike- it's quite shocking she is still invited to events like these, but, you know. A little bit of the right kind of scandal is always fun.”

entities.title: Screams in Montague's Attic
entities.content: “[b]Lord Montague[/b]’s wife is said to have died years ago. But a serving girl swore she heard all matter of unholy screams coming from the attic of that great house. He must have locked her up there, all alone, for years and years now… the mind boggles.”

entities.title: Sharpe and Lewis Sitting In A Tree
entities.content: I saw [b]Miss Adrianne Sharpe[/b] in the company of the honourable [b]Captain Silas Lewis[/b]. If he's courting her, then she really must be something special...

entities.title: Sharpe Seamstress
entities.content: “Why is old [b]Miss Hester Sharpe[/b] here, considering how her family fell from grace decades ago? Why, she’s said to be an incredible seamstress - she probably made a dress for someone important. Do you suppose she’s taking commissions?”

entities.title: Sharpe Tempers
entities.content: “I never knew that Captain Sharpe married while he was posted in India, but with his sister, [b]Miss Sharpe[/b]’s, situation, I suppose it’s not all that surprising we never heard the news. I wouldn’t be surprised if he never even wrote to her about it. I bet she didn’t even know he had a daughter until [b]Miss Adrianne[/b] arrived on her doorstep. A bitter spinster and a sour child, what a pair the two of them make indeed.”

entities.title: Sheridan Collapses on Waldock
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Sheridan Lancaster[/b] collapsed a few weeks ago after a dance with [b]Miss Waldock[/b]. I doubt her company was so entrancing that it laid him out. That boy is incredibly frail. He’ll be lucky to see thirty, I’m afraid. I do wonder if it runs in the family...”

entities.title: Sing Sweet Rosaline
entities.content: “[b]Miss Paciello[/b] has the sweetest singing voice I’ve ever heard, like an angel in flight. There’s no wonder she’s one of the foremost opera stars in England. It’s an honor just to be in that audience, each and every night. No, I never miss one when Rosaline performs...”

entities.title: Sir Lucian Saves Willoughby
entities.content: I heard [b]Sir Lucian St. John-Smith[/b] saved [b]Mrs. Augusta Willougby[/b] from certain demise, pulling her from the path of an oncoming carriage! How gallant...

entities.title: Sir Servant
entities.content: “I heard [b]Sir Rawdon Nash[/b] tries to do the servants work around the Nash estate. Isn't it absurd? Well, you can't turn a farmhand into a lord, no matter what he wears.”

entities.title: Spencer's Many Suitors
entities.content: “[b]Miss Spencer[/b] has been entertaining half a dozen suitors. Among their number are [b]Sir Rawdon Nash[/b], [b]Lt. Foxsmith[/b], and [b]Captain Thompson[/b].”

entities.title: Spencer's Wandering Eye
entities.content: “[b]Lt. Spencer[/b] is engaged to [b]Miss Harrington[/b]. Which would be all right and good, if he didn’t so often make eyes at [b]Miss Wickham[/b].”

entities.title: Spinster Sharpe
entities.content: “[b]Miss Hester Sharpe[/b] used to be a member of the ton but had a child out of wedlock and retreated entirely from society. It’s been so long that no one really remembers it, and she’s well-off enough now it doesn’t matter, but why has she come back to balls all of a sudden?”

entities.title: Spoilt Sharpe
entities.content: “Sharpe is an apt name for her. [b]Miss Adrianne Sharpe[/b] is nothing but sharp edges. That’s a child who was spoilt rotten in India, you mark my words.”

entities.title: St John-Who?
entities.content: “I’d never heard of [b]Sir Lucian St. John-Smith[/b] until a few years ago. He hails from the St. John-Smiths of Kent, so I’ve been told, and made his way to prominence by being something of an art connoisseur.”

entities.title: Steele and Thompson, BFFs
entities.content: “[b]Miss Thompson[/b], the trading company heiress, has been a frequent companion of [b]Miss Steele[/b], the rugged nobody from up the coast, since her boat arrived in England. They seem like odd bedfellows, don’t they?”

entities.title: Steele-y Dealer
entities.content: “I swear I recognize [b]Miss Steele[/b] from somewhere. Did I see her dealing cards at the Star & Garter once?”

entities.title: Steeley Bastard
entities.content: “[b]Miss Sophia Steele[/b] is someone’s bastard, no matter how you dress her up or educate her. She might do for some second son, but she’s as coarse as a dock worker herself, and I can’t imagine she’ll catch the attention of many eligible young men.”

entities.title: Sweet Major Osbourne
entities.content: “I’ve never met a more kind, generous man than [b]Major James Osbourne[/b]. He’s quiet, mind you, but he dined with us last year, just once, before reporting to his unit for drills. I believe my son may have mentioned, in passing, how badly he’d always wanted a rocking horse, done up like a member of the regiment. And when he visited again last week? What did Major Osbourne bring than that exact toy, quite as the boy described it. We’d all forgotten, but not Major Osbourne!”

entities.title: Tatiana's No Place Like Home
entities.content: “[b]@[Lady Tatiana Dostov](person:0f247b43-430f-4568-b885-ff434e1ef4e6)'s[/b] mother wants her to find a husband, but who wants to marry someone who would rather live in the backwater of Saint Petersburg, or worse, move you with her...?”

entities.title: Tea for Miss Bennington
entities.content: “Since she’s returned from the continent, [b]Miss Bennington[/b] has been quite welcomed into polite society. I heard she merited an invitation to the Bloomsbury Travelling Tea. That elite ladies’ circle has denied [b]Mrs. Middleton[/b] admission for decades.”

entities.title: The Middleton Deerhounds
entities.content: “The [b]Middleton[/b] family is poor, but they breed an incredible line of Scottish Deerhounds. There could be money in it, if they were pedigreed. Didn’t the old Duke of Devonshire used to breed the dogs?”

entities.title: The Thompson Inheritance
entities.content: “The Kingston Trading Company is fabulously profitable and there’s exactly one man who stands to inherit it - [b]Captain Barnabas Thompson[/b]. Mark my words, you can keep your Dukes and Lords, but the lady that catches Captain Thompson will be a happy wife indeed. He’s the true treasure here tonight.”

entities.title: Thieving Middletons
entities.content: “The Middletons stole over five hundred pounds from the Radcliffes years ago, that’s why their families are always fighting…”

entities.title: Thieving Whitney
entities.content: “I heard [b]Mrs. Huxley[/b] is a compulsive thief. She stole some silver right off the table at someone’s formal tea, so it’s said. I don’t remember who’s tea!”

entities.title: Thompson and Willoughby Duplicate Dresses
entities.content: “Did you hear that [b]Miss Thompson[/b], the Kingston Trading Company heiress, wore the same gown as [b]Miss Willoughby[/b] to the Fairweather ball at the beginning of the season? How mortifying. Miss Thompson clearly copied Miss Willoughby’s choices.”

entities.title: Too Early Morning Races Martindale and Priestly
entities.content: I heard [b]Mr. Geoffrey Priestly[/b] say that he raced [b]Mr. Anthony Martindale[/b] in Hyde Park at dawn more than once when he was coming home late. What kind of gentleman gets up that early? Maybe there’s something wrong with him.”

entities.title: Tournelle the Cunning
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Tournelle Lancaster[/b] is a man of no family, no background, but gods is he shrewd. I heard he persuaded the former Lord Davenport to finance his education on sheer willpower alone. Dreadfully clever with sums, despite his rough upbringing.“

entities.title: Tournelle, Banished!
entities.content: “I’ve heard there was some scandal years ago between [b]Miss Willoughby[/b], [b]Mr. Willoughby[/b], and [b]Mr. Tournelle[/b]. Had Mr. Tournelle found himself courting Miss Willoughby? Had he stolen something from them? Either way he was summarily dismissed from the Nash family home, where he had been staying. Or was it the Davenports...”

entities.title: Tournelle's Nash for Romance
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Tournelle[/b] and his foster sister, [b]Miss Elizabeth[/b], seem altogether too close for comfort.”

entities.title: Unreliable Bruce Willoughby
entities.content: “A unreliable character, [b]Mr. Bruce Willoughby[/b]. Though he comes from good stock, he is nearly impossible to have as a guest at various social functions. He’ll claim he’s forgotten, but I think frankly it’s just plain old bad manners.”

entities.title: Uppercut Cavill
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Cavill[/b] was the premiere boxer during his days at University, and though he’s a lawyer, don’t doubt he’d be a formidable foe to any man who stood against him.”

entities.title: Victoria Verbally Spars Tournelle
entities.content: “Headstrong and opinionated, [b]Miss Victoria Middleton[/b] is. I’ve heard she’s been butting heads lately with [b]Mr. Tournelle[/b] and that their fights can be truly legendary.”

entities.title: Wetherlocke's Heroic Husband
entities.content: The widow [b]Mrs. Helena Wetherlocke's[/b] husband died a hero! Saving a boy from a carriage, he certainly redeemed himself in the end didn't he? She must be so lonely now, widowed so young.

entities.title: What Of The Waldocks
entities.content: “[b]The Waldocks[/b] need desperately to marry well, poor things. No father, no fortune, no home to speak of. Luckily [b]Miss Charlotte Waldock[/b] is pretty, if not flighty. [b]Miss Julia Waldock[/b], the eldest… well, she’s never expressed interest in any man, has she?”

entities.title: Where's the money?
entities.content: The envelope with the cash is in a book of Kant in Martin Cavill's study.

entities.title: Which Willoughby Wears the Pants
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Willoughby[/b] should be the head of his family someday, but it’s his sister, [b]Miss Willoughby[/b] that really holds the reins.”

entities.title: Whirlwind Engagement for Pippa Pembroke
entities.content: “I heard that [b]Mr. Pembroke[/b], who stands to be a Viscount, married [b]Mrs. Pippa Pembroke[/b] the same day he met her. Now, just because they’d never met before the marriage doesn’t mean there was foul play afoot, but they don’t exactly seem passionately in love, either.”

entities.title: Whitney Radcliffe and Martin Cavill
entities.content: “[b]Mr. Martin Cavill[/b] has been sponsored these many years past by Mrs. Whitney Huxley, who supported his schooling. They’ve no blood relation except that Mrs. Radcliffe was supposedly a friend of his mother’s. I don’t believe that for an instant. He must be her bastard, that [b]Mr. John Radcliffe[/b] refuses to claim.”

entities.title: Why Wentworth Returned
entities.content: “Doctor Hugh Wentworth lived in India with his twin sister, Miss Mary Ann Wentworth. But that poor thing never made it home. With his parents long gone, he really is all alone, isn't he? He must be looking for a wife, to come back from India after all these years...”

entities.title: Wickham Eying Harrington
entities.content: “I’ve never seen something so outrageous as [b]Miss Wickham[/b] making eyes at [b]Mr. Harrington[/b]. She’s some orphan girl from the streets turned governess.”

entities.title: Wickham Hunting Harrington
entities.content: “[b]Miss Wickham[/b] is a resourceful fortune huntress. She’d cast her eyes at [b]Mr. Harrington[/b] not long ago, I wonder who’s next.”

entities.title: Wickham Overshadowing Harrington
entities.content: “@[[b]Miss Harrington[/b]](person:0b7871c8-470e-44a2-adfd-df2563988cd9) is quite compromised by @[[b]Miss Wickham[/b]](person:6741985b-16a9-4206-bc70-7218ff09b56d). Miss Wickham much overshadows her in beauty, wit, and grace. I doubt Miss Harrington will ever escape from her shadow.”

entities.title: Willoughby and the Cad Captain
entities.content: “@[[b]Mr. Willoughby[/b]](person:90d47ebf-ef1f-4a8a-b2e5-b205034a6e53) has been cursing @[[b]Captain Thompson[/b]](person:1b5f78ae-c59b-4fa6-8cee-b69606c9f3dd)’s name the last few weeks, and I’ve heard might challenge him to a duel – Mr. Willoughby is so gentle, I wonder what on earth he’s done.”

entities.title: Willoughby and Thompson Match Gowns
entities.content: “@[[b]Miss Willoughby[/b]](person:df302dbc-d965-4444-876d-a88248a7063e) wore a matching gown to @[[b]Miss Thompson[/b]](person:f1d29e0d-c70b-4c29-8338-d70578f5bea9) at the premiere ball of the season- how embarrassing! She must be particularly cross about it, as Miss Willoughby considers herself something of a woman of fashion.”

entities.title: Willoughby Snares a Foxsmith
entities.content: “I’ve heard that @[[b]Miss Willoughby[/b]](person:df302dbc-d965-4444-876d-a88248a7063e) and @[[b]Lt. Foxsmith[/b]](person:71777950-6f6e-41ce-896c-ce17f10a5752) became engaged this month past. He’s far below her in wealth and status, but stranger things have happened I suppose. @[[b]Lord Collin Davenport[/b]](person:9c8d167e-e8b4-42a4-9c23-3b2af707de87) is positively stewing about it, as it has long been assumed he and Miss Willoughby would wed someday.”

entities.title: Young Miss Sharpe's Company
entities.content: “Quite the catch, @[[b]Miss Adrianne Sharpe[/b]](person:cec0b747-8c34-42a7-ad3e-9f87f4826597). I heard from @[[b]Mr. Harrington[/b]](person:4d6031ee-b918-44a8-b4fc-92222113a6d4) that her father was an incredibly wealthy man with the East India Company, and now that he’s dead she stands to inherit his whole fortune. She only inherits once she’s married, of course, due to some stipulation in the will- so she’s eager to do so, now that she’s in England. Born in Calcutta- her grandmother on her father’s side was Indian, so I’m told. This must all be incredibly new to her. And now to have to live with the dreadful spinster @[[b]Miss Hester Sharpe[/b]](person:da9cc467-54f1-4d2c-9371-e6790135beb2)!”
